congregations.name: Shalom in Messiah Congregation
congregations.description: A Messianic Jewish congregation focused on Jewish-Christian reconciliation and outreach in South Los Angeles.
congregations.notes: Primary weekly Shabbat service on Friday evenings at 7:00 PM.

congregations.name: Beth Shalom Messianic Fellowship
congregations.description: Messianic congregation serving Jewish and Gentile believers in the Pasadena area.
congregations.notes: Erev Shabbat service Fridays at 6:30 PM; Torah service Saturday mornings.

congregations.name: Olive Tree Christian Fellowship
congregations.description: A church highlighting the Jewish roots of the Christian faith with a strong emphasis on Israel.
congregations.notes: Monthly Friday evening Israel-focused service at 7:30 PM.

congregations.name: Hope of Israel Ministry Center
congregations.description: Interdenominational center equipping believers to share Messiah with the Jewish community.
congregations.notes: Hebraic worship service on Shabbat mornings at 10:00 AM.

congregations.name: New Covenant Messianic Congregation
congregations.description: Messianic congregation in the San Fernando Valley with Hebrew worship and teaching.
congregations.notes: Family-friendly Erev Shabbat at 7:00 PM with fellowship meal afterward.

congregations.name: Shema Israel Fellowship
congregations.description: Congregation near Anaheim focused on outreach to Israeli tourists and workers.
congregations.notes: Outreach-focused Havdalah service on Saturday evenings.

congregations.name: Jerusalem Way Christian Center
congregations.description: Church in San Diego teaching through the Bible with an emphasis on Israel and the Jewish people.
congregations.notes: Monthly Israel prayer focus on Sunday evenings.

congregations.name: Zion Hope Messianic Congregation
congregations.description: Messianic congregation in Manhattan with strong Jewish-Christian outreach and teaching.
congregations.notes: Downtown Erev Shabbat service with Hebrew and English worship.

congregations.name: Galilee Messianic Church
congregations.description: Sydney-based fellowship connecting Jewish and Gentile believers in Yeshua.
congregations.notes: Hebraic-style worship service on Saturdays at 10:30 AM.

congregations.name: Beth Chaim Messianic Assembly
congregations.description: Toronto-based congregation focused on teaching the Jewish roots of Christian faith.
congregations.notes: Additional Hebrew class on weekday evenings.

congregations.name: Peace of Jerusalem Fellowship
congregations.description: Chicago congregation praying for the peace of Jerusalem and sharing Messiah with the Jewish community.
congregations.notes: Monthly Israel intercession gathering on Saturday mornings.

congregations.name: Light of Zion Fellowship
congregations.notes: Congregation closed in 2024; retained in system for historical purposes.

events.title: Evening Online Bible Study: Gospel of John
events.subtitle: Exploring the Jewish context of Yeshua's miracles
events.description: A 6-week online Bible study walking through key sections of the Gospel of John with an emphasis on its Jewish background and Messianic themes.

events.title: Online Bible Study: Acts Through Jewish Eyes
events.subtitle: Pentecost and the birth of the early Messianic community
events.description: A one-night deep dive into Acts 1–2, exploring the Jewish context of Shavuot (Pentecost) and the outpouring of the Spirit in Jerusalem.

events.title: Romans: The Gospel to Jew and Gentile
events.subtitle: Hybrid Bible study from a Messianic perspective
events.description: A 4-week hybrid Bible study covering Romans 1–8, taught live in Los Angeles and streamed online for remote participants.

events.title: Feasts of the Lord Conference
events.subtitle: Understanding God's calendar and prophetic plan
events.description: A weekend conference exploring the biblical feasts, their fulfillment in Messiah, and their relevance for Jewish and Gentile believers today.

events.title: Weekly Online Prayer for Israel
events.subtitle: Standing in the gap for Jerusalem and the Jewish people
events.description: Join intercessors from around the world in a weekly online prayer meeting focused on Israel, the Jewish people, and the Middle East.

events.title: Holocaust Survivors Outreach Training
events.subtitle: Equipping believers to serve with sensitivity and honor
events.description: A practical seminar on ministering to Holocaust survivors, including trauma awareness, cultural sensitivity, and sharing Messiah with compassion.

events.title: Passover Street Outreach
events.subtitle: Sharing Messiah in the Passover season
events.description: Join our local team as we distribute Passover booklets and engage in conversations about the Lamb of God in a Jewish neighborhood.

events.title: Weekly Shabbat Worship Service
events.subtitle: Messianic worship and Torah teaching
events.description: Join us each week for a Shabbat worship service featuring Hebrew and English praise, Torah readings, and teaching from a Messianic perspective.

events.title: Messiah in the Feasts of Israel
events.subtitle: Completed online Bible study series
events.description: A past 8-week Bible study exploring how Yeshua fulfills the Feasts of the Lord.

events.title: Middle East & Israel Live Update
events.subtitle: Current events and prayer points
events.description: A monthly webinar providing a live update from our team in Israel, with prayer points and practical ways to stand with the Jewish people.

events.title: Yom HaShoah Memorial Service
events.subtitle: Remembering the six million and honoring survivors
events.description: An online memorial service for Yom HaShoah, featuring survivor stories, Scripture readings, and guided prayer.

events.title: Internal Staff Retreat (Draft)
events.subtitle: Planning and prayer for the coming year
events.description: Internal planning retreat for ministry staff. Not visible to the public.

fund_designations.name: General Ministry Fund
fund_designations.description: Supports the overall work of the ministry, including staff, operations, and core programs.

fund_designations.name: Holocaust Survivor Outreach
fund_designations.description: Provides humanitarian aid, home visits, and Gospel-centered care for Holocaust survivors in Israel and the diaspora.

fund_designations.name: Israel Relief & Compassion Fund
fund_designations.description: Emergency relief and ongoing assistance for families in Israel affected by conflict and poverty.

fund_designations.name: Digital Media Ministry
fund_designations.description: Expands our online teaching, live streams, and evangelistic media in Hebrew, English, and Russian.

fund_designations.name: Student Outreach Fund
fund_designations.description: Equips and sends teams to reach Jewish students on university campuses with the message of Messiah.

fund_designations.name: Hebrew Bible & Literature Distribution
fund_designations.description: Prints and distributes Hebrew Scriptures and Messianic literature to seekers worldwide.

newsletter_topics.name: Weekly Israel Updates
newsletter_topics.description: A weekly email with news from Israel, field reports, and current prayer points for the Jewish people.

newsletter_topics.name: Monthly Teaching Articles
newsletter_topics.description: Monthly in-depth Bible teachings on Jewish-Christian relations, the Jewish roots of faith, and end-time themes.

newsletter_topics.name: Holocaust Survivor Outreach Updates
newsletter_topics.description: Periodic updates highlighting stories and needs from our Holocaust survivor outreach projects.

newsletter_topics.name: Festivals and Feasts
newsletter_topics.description: Seasonal emails explaining the biblical feasts and offering resources for celebrating them in Messiah.

newsletter_topics.name: General Ministry News
newsletter_topics.description: Occasional updates about major ministry milestones, events, and opportunities to get involved.

products.title: Discovering the Jewish Feasts
products.subtitle: A Christian Guide to God's Appointed Times
products.description: An accessible introduction to the Jewish feasts—Passover, Shavuot, Rosh Hashanah, Yom Kippur, Sukkot, and more—showing how each points to Messiah and how believers today can celebrate them.
products.author: Sarah Levy

products.title: Messiah in the Feasts of Israel
products.subtitle: Seeing Yeshua in the Biblical Holidays
products.description: This classic study walks through each Jewish feast to reveal the redemptive work of Messiah. Ideal for small group studies and personal devotion.
products.author: David Rosen

products.title: Celebrating Biblical Feasts with Jesus
products.subtitle: Family Devotions for Jewish Feasts and Festivals
products.description: A practical, family-friendly guide with devotions, prayers, and activities for celebrating the Jewish feasts in a Christ-centered way.
products.author: Rachel Stein

products.title: Family Guide to the Feasts of the Lord
products.subtitle: Teaching Children the Jewish Feasts
products.description: Helps parents teach children about the Jewish feasts through simple explanations, crafts, and discussion questions that point to Yeshua.
products.author: Jonathan Goldman

products.title: Jewish Feasts and the Early Church
products.subtitle: Recovering the Apostolic Pattern
products.description: A historical and biblical study of how the earliest Jewish and Gentile believers related to the feasts of Israel.
products.author: Dr. Hannah Cohen

products.title: Seasons of the Messiah
products.subtitle: Exploring the Jewish Feasts and God's Prophetic Calendar
products.description: Shows how the cycle of Jewish feasts reveals God's plan of redemption and the return of Messiah.
products.author: Michael Ben-Ami

products.title: Roots of Faith
products.subtitle: Understanding the Jewish Roots of Christianity
products.description: A foundational resource on the Jewish roots of Christian faith, ideal for small groups and personal study.
products.author: Daniel Katz

products.title: Passover and the Gospel
products.subtitle: A Witnessing Booklet for Jewish Friends
products.description: A short evangelistic booklet showing how the Passover story points to the Lamb of God, written sensitively for Jewish readers.
products.author: Outreach Team

products.title: Israel and the Church
products.subtitle: Why Jewish-Christian Relations Matter
products.description: A concise overview of God's ongoing purposes for Israel and how the Church can relate in love and humility.
products.author: Mark Levine

products.title: Messianic Prophecies Study Guide (PDF)
products.subtitle: Key Old Testament Passages About the Messiah
products.description: Downloadable PDF walking through 30 key Messianic prophecies with discussion questions and application points.
products.author: Teaching Team

products.title: Support an Israeli Family
products.subtitle: Care Package and Gospel Resources
products.description: Provide a care package to an Israeli family in need, including food vouchers and Messianic literature in Hebrew and Russian.
included_items: Food voucher for Israeli supermarket (equivalent of $40)

products.title: Student Outreach Pack
products.subtitle: Reaching Jewish Students on Campus
products.description: Equip a campus outreach team with printed materials and small gifts to share the love of Messiah with Jewish students.
included_items: Pack of 50 invite cards with QR code to campus Bible study
Reusable canvas tote bag for materials

products.title: Holocaust Survivor Care Package
products.subtitle: Honor a Survivor with Practical Love
products.description: Provide food, medication assistance, and a handwritten card to a Holocaust survivor in Israel through our local team.
included_items: Handwritten greeting card delivered by our staff

products.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah (Audio Set)
products.subtitle: 4-Part Teaching Series on CD
products.description: Audio recordings of a four-part series through Romans 1–8, focusing on Jew and Gentile in Messiah.
products.author: Pastor David Cohen

products.title: Feasts of Israel: A Visual Journey (DVD)
products.subtitle: Experience the Jewish Feasts in Israel
products.description: Travel with our team through Israel as we celebrate the feasts and explain their prophetic significance.

products.title: 7-Day Messianic Prophecies Devotional (Digital)
products.subtitle: Daily Readings on the Coming Messiah
products.description: A 7-day devotional plan walking through key Messianic prophecies with reflections and prayers.
products.author: Teaching Team

devotional_plans.title: 7-Day Messianic Prophecies Journey
devotional_plans.subtitle: Seeing Yeshua in the Law, Prophets, and Writings
devotional_plans.description: A focused 7-day devotional walking through key Messianic prophecies from Genesis to Malachi. Each day features a primary Old Testament passage, its New Testament fulfillment, a short reflection, and a guided prayer for the Jewish people to know their Messiah.

devotional_plans.title: The Names of the Messiah
devotional_plans.subtitle: 14 Days in the Messianic Prophecies of Isaiah
devotional_plans.description: Spend two weeks in Isaiah’s Messianic prophecies, exploring titles like Immanuel, Prince of Peace, and the Servant of the Lord. Devotions highlight how these promises are fulfilled in Yeshua and how to pray for Jewish friends to encounter Him.

devotional_plans.title: Road to Emmaus
devotional_plans.subtitle: 21 Days in the Messianic Hope of Israel
devotional_plans.description: A three-week devotional following the pattern of Yeshua’s teaching on the road to Emmaus: "Moses and all the Prophets." Trace the unfolding promise of the Messiah and learn to share these prophecies naturally with Jewish and Gentile seekers.

devotional_plans.title: Rhythms of Shabbat
devotional_plans.subtitle: 10 Days Discovering the Gift of Rest
devotional_plans.description: An introduction to the meaning of Shabbat and how followers of Yeshua can embrace God’s rhythm of rest. Daily readings include Scripture, a short teaching on Jewish practice, and practical suggestions for honoring Shabbat as a witness to Jewish friends.

devotional_plans.title: Covenants of Promise
devotional_plans.subtitle: 14-Day Study in the Covenants of Israel
devotional_plans.description: Walk through the major biblical covenants—Abrahamic, Mosaic, Davidic, and New Covenant—and see how they reveal God’s faithfulness to Israel and the nations. Includes reflection questions suitable for small-group use.

devotional_plans.title: Praying for the Peace of Jerusalem
devotional_plans.subtitle: 7 Days of Intercession for Israel
devotional_plans.description: A one-week guide to praying biblically and sensitively for Jerusalem, the Jewish people worldwide, and God’s purposes in the Middle East. Each day features specific prayer points and a short story from our field teams.

devotional_plans.title: Streams in the Psalms
devotional_plans.subtitle: 30 Days of Refreshing from Israel’s Prayer Book
devotional_plans.description: Journey through selected psalms with an eye toward both personal renewal and God’s heart for Israel and the nations. Ideal for individuals or prayer groups seeking deeper intimacy with the Lord.

devotional_plans.title: Passover and the Lamb of God
devotional_plans.subtitle: 10-Day Devotional on Redemption
devotional_plans.description: Explore the story of the Exodus and the Last Supper to see how Yeshua fulfills Passover. Includes simple ideas for celebrating Passover in a way that honors Jewish tradition and testifies to Messiah.

devotional_plans.title: Trumpets, Atonement, and Tabernacles
devotional_plans.subtitle: 14 Days in the Fall Feasts of Israel
devotional_plans.description: A two-week devotional on the prophetic significance of Yom Teruah, Yom Kippur, and Sukkot, with daily readings that spotlight repentance, atonement, and the hope of God dwelling with His people.

devotional_plans.title: One New Man in Messiah
devotional_plans.subtitle: 15 Days on Unity Between Jew and Gentile
devotional_plans.description: A devotional exploration of Ephesians 2–3 and related passages, unpacking God’s plan to create one new man in Messiah and how that shapes Jewish-Christian relationships today.

devotional_plans.title: Hope in Suffering
devotional_plans.subtitle: 7 Days of Comfort from Jewish and Christian Voices
devotional_plans.description: Hear stories of Holocaust survivors, Israeli believers, and followers of Yeshua worldwide who have found hope in the midst of suffering. Each day pairs Scripture with a short testimony and prayer focus.

devotional_days.title: The First Promise of a Redeemer
devotional_days.summary: God promises a coming Seed who will crush the serpent, planting the first Messianic hope in Israel’s story.
devotional_days.content:
Read Genesis 3:15 slowly. This verse, often called the protoevangelium, is the first hint of the Gospel. God speaks of the Seed of the woman who will crush the serpent’s head, even as His own heel is bruised. The language is mysterious, but it introduces a coming Deliverer who will defeat evil at great personal cost.

Consider how this promise would echo through the generations of Israel: a promised Seed, born of a woman, who would one day undo the damage of the fall. Paul alludes to this hope in Romans 16:20, assuring believers that the God of peace will soon crush Satan under their feet in Messiah.

Prayer: Thank the Lord for planting hope at the very moment of humanity’s failure. Pray for Jewish people today to see in this ancient promise the shadow of the Messiah who has come and will come again.

devotional_days.title: The Seed of Abraham and Blessing to the Nations
devotional_days.summary: God chooses Abraham and promises a Seed through whom all the families of the earth will be blessed.
devotional_days.content:
In Genesis 12:1–3, God calls Abram and promises that all the families of the earth will be blessed through him. Later, this promise is narrowed to his Seed (see Genesis 22:18). Paul explains in Galatians 3 that this Seed ultimately points to Messiah.

Israel’s calling was never only for herself; from the beginning, God’s covenant with Abraham had the nations in view. Messiah, the ultimate Seed of Abraham, would bring blessing and salvation to Jews and Gentiles together.

Prayer: Thank God for His covenant faithfulness to Abraham and his descendants. Ask Him to open the eyes of both Jewish and Gentile hearts to see Yeshua as the promised Seed who brings blessing to all nations.

devotional_days.title: The Lion from the Tribe of Judah
devotional_days.summary: Jacob foretells a ruler from Judah whose scepter will not depart, pointing to Messiah’s royal authority.
devotional_days.content:
As Jacob blesses his sons, he speaks prophetically over Judah: a ruler will come from his line, and the scepter will not depart from him. This royal promise anticipates a kingly Messiah arising from the tribe of Judah.

In Revelation 5:5, Yeshua is called the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the One worthy to open the scroll. The hope of a righteous, everlasting King for Israel and the nations finds its fulfillment in Him.

Prayer: Praise Yeshua as the Lion of Judah and rightful King of Israel. Pray for Jewish people to encounter their promised King in the pages of Scripture and in the lives of those who follow Him.

devotional_days.title: The Prophet Like Moses
devotional_days.summary: Moses foretells a future Prophet whom Israel must hear, fulfilled in Yeshua’s teaching and authority.
devotional_days.content:
Moses tells Israel that God will raise up a Prophet like him from among their brothers. The people are commanded to listen to Him, and God warns of consequences for rejecting His words.

Peter, preaching at the Temple in Acts 3, identifies Yeshua as this promised Prophet. Like Moses, He mediates a new covenant, performs signs and wonders, and speaks God’s words faithfully.

Prayer: Ask the Lord to help you listen to Yeshua, the Prophet like Moses. Intercede for Jewish friends, that they would consider His teachings afresh and recognize His unique authority.

devotional_days.title: The Suffering Servant
devotional_days.summary: Isaiah paints a portrait of a Servant who suffers and bears the sins of many as a substitutionary sacrifice.
devotional_days.content:
Isaiah 52:13–53:6 gives one of the clearest pictures of Messiah’s suffering in all of Scripture. The Servant is despised and rejected, pierced for our transgressions, and crushed for our iniquities. The Lord lays on Him the iniquity of us all.

Peter quotes this passage to describe Yeshua’s atoning death, affirming that by His wounds we are healed. Jewish and Christian readers alike are invited to ask: Who is this Servant, and what has He done for me?

Prayer: Thank Yeshua for bearing your sin and shame on the cross. Pray that many Jewish people would read Isaiah 53 with open hearts and encounter the Suffering Servant as their Messiah.

devotional_days.title: The New Covenant with Israel
devotional_days.summary: God promises a New Covenant with the house of Israel and Judah, written on the heart and grounded in forgiveness.
devotional_days.content:
Jeremiah prophesies a New Covenant—not to replace Israel, but given to the house of Israel and Judah. This covenant includes an internalized Torah, intimate knowledge of God, and complete forgiveness of sin.

At the Last Supper, Yeshua lifts the cup and declares it to be the New Covenant in His blood. The promise to Israel becomes the gateway of salvation for the nations, as Jews and Gentiles share together in this covenant through Messiah.

Prayer: Thank God for the New Covenant written on the heart. Pray for Jewish people worldwide to discover that this covenant, promised to them, has been inaugurated by their own Messiah.

devotional_days.title: The Coming King of Zion
devotional_days.summary: A humble King comes to Jerusalem, bringing salvation and peace to the nations.
devotional_days.content:
Zechariah calls Zion to rejoice because her King is coming—humble and riding on a donkey. He will proclaim peace to the nations, and His rule will extend to the ends of the earth.

The Gospels present Yeshua’s entry into Jerusalem as the fulfillment of this prophecy. The crowds cry, “Hosanna!” yet many still do not recognize the nature of His kingship.

Prayer: Celebrate Yeshua as the humble King of Zion and Lord of all. Ask God to prepare hearts in Jerusalem and around the world to welcome Him at His return, and to use you as a witness to His kingship.

devotional_days.title: Praying Psalm 122 for Jerusalem
devotional_days.summary: Begin by praying Scripture’s call to seek the peace of Jerusalem and the good of God’s people.
devotional_days.content:
Psalm 122 invites us to rejoice in going up to the house of the Lord and to pray for the peace of Jerusalem. This peace (shalom) is more than the absence of war; it is wholeness, safety, and flourishing under God’s rule.

As you pray this psalm, remember that Jerusalem is both a real city with real people and a symbol of God’s purposes in the earth. Your prayers join generations of Jewish and Christian believers who have carried this burden before the Lord.

Prayer Focus: Ask God to bring true shalom to Jerusalem—peace with Him through Messiah, peace between communities, and peace on her streets.

devotional_days.title: God’s Covenant Love for Israel
devotional_days.summary: Anchor your prayers in God’s unshakable covenant commitment to Israel.
devotional_days.content:
Jeremiah 31 links God’s covenant with Israel to the enduring order of the sun, moon, and stars. Paul echoes this in Romans 11, insisting that God has not rejected His people whom He foreknew.

When we pray for Israel, we are agreeing with God’s own heart and promises. This guards us from pride or despair and keeps our intercession rooted in Scripture rather than headlines alone.

Prayer Focus: Thank God for His faithfulness to Israel across history. Ask Him to reveal His covenant love to both religious and secular Jewish people today.

devotional_days.title: Salvation for the Jewish People
devotional_days.summary: Pray in agreement with Paul’s longing for Israel’s salvation and God’s promise of a future turning.
devotional_days.content:
Paul confesses that his heart’s desire and prayer to God is for Israel to be saved. Later he speaks of a future moment when “all Israel will be saved” as the Deliverer turns ungodliness away from Jacob.

Our prayers for Jewish people to know Yeshua are not an imposition, but a sharing in the apostolic burden of love. We long for them to know their own Messiah and the joy of the New Covenant promised to them.

Prayer Focus: Name specific Jewish friends, communities, or cities before the Lord. Ask Him to reveal Yeshua in dreams, Scriptures, and relationships filled with grace and humility.

devotional_days.title: Comfort for Survivors and the Brokenhearted
devotional_days.summary: Seek God’s comfort for Holocaust survivors and those who carry deep trauma and loss.
devotional_days.content:
God commands, “Comfort, comfort My people.” Isaiah 61 describes the Anointed One binding up the brokenhearted and giving beauty for ashes. Many in the Jewish community, especially Holocaust survivors and their families, carry generational pain.

As we pray for Israel, we must also pray for healing from trauma—physical, emotional, and spiritual. Yeshua, the Servant and Messiah, is uniquely able to enter this pain with compassion and hope.

Prayer Focus: Pray for Holocaust survivors in Israel and around the world, and for ministries that serve them. Ask God to reveal Messiah’s comfort in ways that honor their stories and dignity.

devotional_days.title: Praying for Leaders and Authorities
devotional_days.summary: Lift up leaders in Israel and surrounding nations, asking God for wisdom and justice.
devotional_days.content:
Scripture urges us to pray for kings and all in authority so that we may live peaceful and quiet lives. Psalm 72 gives language for asking God to grant rulers justice, righteousness, and compassion for the needy.

In the Middle East, decisions made by leaders can quickly affect the safety and daily life of millions. Our prayers matter as we ask God to guide leaders toward decisions that promote life, truth, and genuine peace.

Prayer Focus: Pray by name for Israeli leaders, local mayors, community heads, and key decision-makers in neighboring nations. Ask God to surround them with wise counsel and restrain those who promote violence or hatred.

devotional_days.title: Unity in the Body of Messiah
devotional_days.summary: Ask the Lord to deepen unity between Jewish and Gentile believers as a witness to the world.
devotional_days.content:
Paul teaches that Yeshua is our peace, making Jew and Gentile one and breaking down the dividing wall of hostility. Yeshua Himself prayed that His followers would be one so that the world would believe the Father sent Him.

In Israel and around the globe, Jewish and Gentile believers often worship separately and sometimes misunderstand one another. Yet God’s plan is a united Body that displays His reconciling power.

Prayer Focus: Pray for Messianic congregations and churches to grow in mutual honor and partnership. Ask God to heal historical wounds and raise up “one new man” communities that model His heart.

devotional_days.title: The Hope of Yeshua’s Return
devotional_days.summary: End the week by fixing your hope on Messiah’s return and Israel’s future turning to Him.
devotional_days.content:
Zechariah envisions a day when the house of David and the inhabitants of Jerusalem will look on the One they have pierced and mourn for Him. Yeshua connects His return to a future moment when Jerusalem will say, “Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord.”

Our prayers for Israel are ultimately shaped by this hope: a future national turning to Messiah and His glorious return. This hope fuels perseverance when circumstances seem dark.

Prayer Focus: Ask God to keep your heart anchored in the promise of Yeshua’s coming. Pray that Jewish people in Jerusalem and worldwide would be prepared to welcome Him with joy.

devotional_days.title: Remembering the Exodus
devotional_days.summary: Begin by recalling God’s mighty deliverance of Israel from Egypt, the foundation of Passover.
devotional_days.content:
Passover begins with God’s command to remember the night He brought Israel out of Egypt. The lamb’s blood on the doorposts marked every home that trusted His word. This story shapes Jewish identity to this day and reveals God as Redeemer and Deliverer.

As followers of Yeshua, we honor this historic deliverance even as we see in it a foreshadowing of a greater redemption. Take time to read Exodus 12 and picture the scene through the eyes of an Israelite family preparing for that first Passover night.

Reflection: How does God’s faithfulness to deliver Israel strengthen your faith for His work in your own life and community?

devotional_days.title: The Lamb Without Blemish
devotional_days.summary: Focus on the spotless lamb as a sign pointing to Messiah, our Passover.
devotional_days.content:
God commands that the Passover lamb be without blemish, a male in its first year. Its blood would mark the homes of those under God’s protection. Paul later writes, “Messiah, our Passover lamb, has been sacrificed.”

The image of a spotless lamb prepared carefully by a Jewish family bridges the story of Exodus and the cross of Yeshua. As you meditate on these verses, consider the cost of redemption and the holiness of the One who gave His life for us.

Reflection: In what ways does seeing Yeshua as your Passover Lamb deepen your gratitude and your longing to share Him with Jewish friends?

devotional_days.title: From Slavery to Freedom
devotional_days.summary: Connect Israel’s physical deliverance from slavery with the spiritual freedom Yeshua offers.
devotional_days.content:
In Deuteronomy 6, parents are instructed to tell their children the story of how God brought them out of Egypt with a mighty hand. The Exodus is a story of moving from slavery into freedom and covenant relationship.

Yeshua tells His listeners that everyone who sins is a slave to sin, but the Son can set them truly free. For both Jewish and Gentile believers, Passover becomes a lens for understanding the deeper bondage of sin and the greater liberation found in Him.

Reflection: Where do you still experience slavery to fear, shame, or sin? Invite Yeshua, the greater Deliverer, to lead you more fully into His freedom.

media_sessions.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah – Part 1: The Gospel to Jew First
media_sessions.description: Pastor David introduces the book of Romans, exploring Paul’s heart for his own Jewish people and the wider Gentile world, and unpacks what it means that the Gospel is “to the Jew first and also to the Greek.”

media_sessions.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah – Part 2: All Have Sinned
media_sessions.description: This session walks through Romans 2–3, showing that both religious Jews and Gentiles stand in need of God’s righteousness, which is revealed apart from the Law yet witnessed by the Law and the Prophets.

media_sessions.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah – Part 3: Abraham, Faith, and Justification
media_sessions.description: Pastor David shows how Paul uses Abraham as the model of justification by faith for both Jew and Gentile, and explains why this matters for Jewish-Christian relations today.

media_sessions.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah – Part 4: Grafted In Together
media_sessions.description: The final part of the series focuses on Romans 9–11, exploring God’s faithfulness to Israel, the mystery of the olive tree, and what it means for Gentile believers to be grafted in without arrogance.

media_sessions.title: Isaiah’s Servant Songs – Part 1: Behold My Servant
media_sessions.description: An introduction to the Servant Songs of Isaiah, examining Isaiah 42 and God’s call for His Servant to be a light to the nations while remaining deeply connected to Israel.

media_sessions.title: Isaiah’s Servant Songs – Part 2: The Suffering Servant
media_sessions.description: A careful reading of Isaiah 52:13–53:12, with special attention to Jewish interpretations and the New Testament’s application of this passage to Yeshua.

media_sessions.title: Isaiah’s Servant Songs – Part 3: From Rejection to Glory
media_sessions.description: This message traces the Servant’s path from apparent failure to ultimate vindication, and offers practical guidance for sharing Isaiah 53 with Jewish friends sensitively.

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 1: God’s Appointed Times
media_sessions.description: An overview of Leviticus 23 and the concept of mo’edim—God’s appointed times with His people—laying a foundation for understanding each feast in light of Messiah.

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 2: Passover and Unleavened Bread
media_sessions.description: Explores the historical celebration of Passover in Jewish homes and shows how Yeshua fulfills the imagery of the lamb, the matzah, and the cup of redemption.

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 3: Firstfruits and Shavuot
media_sessions.description: Connects the offering of firstfruits and the giving of the Torah at Sinai with Yeshua’s resurrection and the outpouring of the Spirit at Shavuot (Pentecost).

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 4: The Fall Feasts Begin
media_sessions.description: Introduces Yom Teruah (Feast of Trumpets) and its themes of awakening, repentance, and the prophetic hope of Messiah’s return.

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 5: Yom Kippur and Atonement
media_sessions.description: Examines the Day of Atonement, the High Priest’s ministry, and how Yeshua’s once-for-all sacrifice fulfills and surpasses the Levitical system.

media_sessions.title: Feasts of the Lord – Part 6: Sukkot and the Coming Kingdom
media_sessions.description: Concludes the series by looking at the Feast of Tabernacles as a prophetic picture of God dwelling with His people and the joy of the coming Messianic Kingdom.

media_sessions.title: Foundations of Shabbat – Part 1: Created for Rest
media_sessions.description: Explores the creation account and God’s rest on the seventh day, introducing Shabbat as a gift for Israel and a sign pointing to Messiah’s rest.

media_sessions.title: Foundations of Shabbat – Part 2: Covenant Sign with Israel
media_sessions.description: Looks at Shabbat as a covenant sign given to Israel, with practical insights on how Gentile believers can honor this gift without replacing Israel.

media_sessions.title: Foundations of Shabbat – Part 3: A Witness to Jewish Friends
media_sessions.description: The final message offers practical guidance on letting a rhythm of rest become a bridge for conversation and witness to Jewish friends and neighbors.

media_series.title: Romans: One New Man in Messiah
media_series.subtitle: A 4-Part Journey Through Romans 1–11
media_series.description: In this four-part series, Pastor David Cohen walks through key sections of Romans, highlighting God’s righteousness, the unity of Jew and Gentile in Messiah, and God’s ongoing faithfulness to Israel. Designed for small groups and individuals who want to understand Paul’s letter in its original Jewish context.

media_series.title: Isaiah’s Suffering Servant
media_series.subtitle: Reading Isaiah’s Servant Songs with Jewish Eyes
media_series.description: A three-part teaching series exploring Isaiah’s Servant Songs, with special focus on Isaiah 52–53. The series compares traditional Jewish interpretations with New Testament readings and offers practical insights for sharing these passages with Jewish friends in a sensitive way.

media_series.title: The Feasts of the Lord
media_series.subtitle: God’s Appointed Times and the Messiah
media_series.description: A six-part overview of Leviticus 23 and the biblical feasts—Passover, Unleavened Bread, Firstfruits, Shavuot, Trumpets, Yom Kippur, and Sukkot. Each session explains the historical Jewish celebration and how each feast points prophetically to Yeshua.

media_series.title: Foundations of Shabbat
media_series.subtitle: Rest, Covenant, and Witness
media_series.description: This three-part series introduces the biblical foundation of Shabbat, its role as a covenant sign with Israel, and how followers of Yeshua can embrace a rhythm of rest that becomes a bridge to Jewish friends and neighbors.

media_series.title: Messianic Prophecies Across Scripture
media_series.subtitle: From Genesis to Revelation
media_series.description: An eight-part survey of key Messianic prophecies spanning the Law, Prophets, and Writings, and their fulfillment in the life, death, and resurrection of Yeshua. Ideal as a follow-up to a 7-day Messianic prophecies devotional plan.

media_series.title: Jewish Roots of the Church
media_series.subtitle: Recovering the First-Century Context
media_series.description: A five-part series exploring the Jewish background of Jesus, the apostles, and the early ekklesia. Topics include the synagogue, Second Temple Judaism, and how understanding these roots deepens our reading of the New Testament.

media_series.title: The Life of Messiah in the Gospel of John
media_series.subtitle: Signs, Feasts, and the Jewish Context of Yeshua
media_series.description: Ten messages walking through the Gospel of John with attention to Jewish feasts, symbols, and idioms. Each session highlights how John presents Yeshua as Israel’s Messiah and Savior of the world.

media_series.title: Praying for Israel and the Jewish People
media_series.subtitle: A Practical Guide to Informed Intercession
media_series.description: A four-part practical series that pairs teaching with guided times of intercession for Israel, the Jewish diaspora, and the Middle East. Complements the 7-day devotional on praying for the peace of Jerusalem.

media_series.title: Hebrew Foundations for Believers
media_series.subtitle: Key Words That Open the Scriptures
media_series.description: A six-part introductory series on basic Hebrew words and concepts—like shalom, hesed, and berit—that deepen our understanding of both Old and New Testaments and help build bridges in Jewish-Christian dialogue.

media_series.title: Romans in Hebrew
media_series.subtitle: Reading Paul’s Letter with Israeli Believers
media_series.description: A three-part discussion-style series in modern Hebrew, designed for Israeli believers and seekers. The teachers read and explain selected passages from Romans, highlighting key terms and addressing common Jewish objections.
